claims.evidence_text: Three levels of salinity of irrigation water were 1, 3 and 5 dS.m . The use of irrigation water with increasing levels of saline led to decrease in the average of production of dry weight of the vegetative part.
claims.claim_summary: Increasing irrigation water salinity from 1 to 5 dS/m significantly reduced wheat vegetative dry matter production.

claims.evidence_text: T1 level was significantly superior to T5 level with dry weight of 14.76 g but it was not significantly superior to the T3 level.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat dry weight at 1 dS/m salinity (14.76 g) was significantly higher than at 5 dS/m but not significantly different from 3 dS/m.

claims.evidence_text: The soil was fertilized with nitrogen @200kgNha in the form of urea fertilizer 46% nitrogen. Nitrogen was added in two batches, day before the date of sowing wheat mixed with the soil and the second after a month from sowing dissolved with irrigation water.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat was fertilized with nitrogen at 200 kg N/ha (urea), split in two applications.

claims.evidence_text: Nitrogen was added in two batches, day before the date of sowing wheat mixed with the soil and the second after a month from sowing dissolved with irrigation water.
claims.claim_summary: Nitrogen for wheat was split: half before sowing and half one month after sowing.

claims.evidence_text: Phosphorus was added @ 100 kg P ha in the form of concentrated superphosphate fertilizer, 20.21% phosphorous, day before sowing, mixed with the soil
claims.claim_summary: Wheat was fertilized with phosphorus at 100 kg P/ha (concentrated superphosphate) before sowing.

claims.evidence_text: Potassium was added at 120 kg ha in the form of potassium sulfate fertilizer 40.43% potassium at once before sowing, mixed with the soil
claims.claim_summary: Wheat was fertilized with potassium at 120 kg/ha (potassium sulfate) before sowing.

claims.evidence_text: Wheat variety Buhooth 22 was sown on November 11, 2020 with 15 seeds per pot and then irrigated with tap water
claims.claim_summary: Wheat (Buhooth 22) was sown at 15 seeds per 5 kg pot, thinned to 10 plants per pot.
The wheat experiment used a clay loam soil (clay 390, silt 317.3, sand 292.7 g/kg).
claims.table_label: Table 1. Chemical and physical properties of soil before planting

claims.claim_summary: The wheat experimental soil had an electrical conductivity of 3.45 dS/m before planting.
claims.table_label: Table 1. Chemical and physical properties of soil before planting
